schema.levels: object with keys 1, 2, 3; each level contains exactly three required main quests

intro.title: Open the Editor Workspace
intro.description: Learn the VS Code workspace basics, then summarize what panels, files, and commands a beginner should recognize first.

1.title: Navigate and Edit Files
1.description: Study basic editing and navigation, then describe how VS Code helps someone move through project files without losing context.

1.title: Use Built-in Source Control
1.description: Learn the Source Control view, then summarize how VS Code exposes changed files, staged files, and commit messages.

1.title: Adjust Basic Settings
1.description: Review settings, then write which editor preferences are safe for personal workflow and which should be shared carefully with a team.

2.title: Debug a Program Flow
2.description: Study debugging concepts, then summarize how breakpoints, variables, and call stacks help find a bug faster than reading code blindly.

2.title: Run a Repeatable Task
2.description: Learn VS Code tasks, then describe a task that would save repeated terminal work in a real project.

2.title: Choose Extensions Deliberately
2.description: Review the extension marketplace, then write a short policy for choosing useful extensions without overloading the editor.

3.title: Organize a Multi-folder Workspace
3.description: Study workspaces, then explain how a multi-folder workspace can support a project with frontend, backend, and documentation areas.

3.title: Create a Profile for a Role
3.description: Learn profiles, then summarize how separate profiles can keep different job contexts or project types from polluting each other.

3.title: Automate Editor Launches
3.description: Review command-line usage, then describe how opening files, folders, or commands from the terminal can speed up troubleshooting.
